verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A (Hsp70-1) is canonically a cytosolic/nuclear inducible chaperone with no TM domain, GPI anchor, or signal peptide. However, it has well-documented stress- and cancer-induced surface translocation: tumor cells display membrane-bound Hsp70 on the outer leaflet (via PS/lipid binding, notably to Gb3/BMP-rich microdomains), and this 'mHsp70' tumor-ecto-pool is the basis of clinical programs (e.g., TKD-peptide-targeting NK-cell therapy, cmHsp70.1 antibody). This is a classic cell-state-induced ecto-chaperone. Not lysosomal exocytosis-dependent in the canonical sense; not a TM-partner-tethered protein; not germline-restricted. Best fit: cell_state_induced (oncogenic transformation + stress).
